audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot golden calf rupture text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for golden calf, Aaron, covenant rupture, Moses intercession, broken tablets, Levite violence, and plague. Strong anti-idolatry and anti-violence safeguards required.

verses.restored: The people saw that Moses delayed to come down from the mountain, and the people gathered against Aaron and said to him, Rise, make for us gods who will go before us, for this Moses, the man who brought us up from land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.
Aaron said to them, Tear off gold rings that are in ears of your wives, sons, and daughters, and bring them to me.
All the people tore off gold rings that were in their ears and brought them to Aaron.
He took from their hand, fashioned it with engraving tool, and made it a molten calf. They said, These are your gods, Israel, who brought you up from land of Egypt.
Aaron saw, and built altar before it; Aaron called out and said, Festival to YHWH tomorrow.
They rose early next day, offered burnt offerings and brought near peace offerings; the people sat to eat and drink, and rose to play.
YHWH spoke to Moses, Go, descend, for your people whom you brought up from land of Egypt have corrupted themselves.

They turned aside quickly from way I commanded them; they made for themselves a molten calf, bowed to it, sacrificed to it, and said, These are your gods, Israel, who brought you up from land of Egypt.
YHWH said to Moses, I have seen this people, and look, it is a stiff-necked people.
Now leave me alone, so my anger may burn against them and consume them, and I will make you into a great nation.
Moses entreated face of YHWH his Divine and said, YHWH, why should your anger burn against your people whom you brought out from land of Egypt with great power and strong hand?
Why should Egypt say, With evil he brought them out, to kill them in mountains and consume them from face of ground? Turn from burning anger and relent concerning harm to your people.

Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, your servants, to whom you swore by yourself and spoke to them: I will multiply your seed like stars of heavens, and all this land I said I will give to your seed, and they shall inherit into the age.
YHWH relented concerning harm that he spoke to do to his people.
Moses turned and descended from mountain, with two tablets of testimony in his hand, tablets written on both sides, from this and from that they were written.
The tablets were work of Divine, and writing was writing of Divine, engraved upon tablets.
Joshua heard voice of people in its noise and said to Moses, Sound of war is in camp.
He said, It is not sound of answering strength, nor sound of answering weakness; sound of singing I hear.
It happened as he came near camp and saw calf and dances, Moses' anger burned; he threw tablets from his hands and shattered them under mountain.
He took calf they had made, burned it with fire, ground it until fine, scattered upon face of waters, and made children of Israel drink.
Moses said to Aaron, What did this people do to you, that you brought upon them great sin/misalignment?
Aaron said, Let not anger of my lord burn. You know the people, that they are in harm.
They said to me, Make for us gods who will go before us; for this Moses, the man who brought us up from land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.
I said to them, Whoever has gold, tear it off. They gave it to me, and I threw it into fire, and this calf came out.
Moses saw the people were let loose, for Aaron had let them loose, for whispering/shame among those rising against them.
Moses stood in gate of camp and said, Who is for YHWH? To me. All sons of Levi gathered to him.
He said to them, Thus says YHWH, Divine of Israel: Each man put sword on thigh; pass through and return from gate to gate in camp, and each kill brother, companion, and neighbor.
Sons of Levi did according to word of Moses, and from the people fell that day about three thousand men.
Moses said, Fill your hand today to YHWH, for each against his son and brother, so blessing may be given upon you today.
It happened on next day Moses said to people, You sinned/misaligned a great sin/misalignment; and now I will go up to YHWH; perhaps I may make atonement for your sin/misalignment.
Moses returned to YHWH and said, Please, this people sinned/misaligned a great sin/misalignment and made for themselves gods of gold.
And now, if you will lift/carry their sin/misalignment—but if not, blot me, please, from your scroll that you wrote.
YHWH said to Moses, Whoever sinned/misaligned against me, I will blot from my scroll.
Now go, lead the people to what I spoke to you. Look, my messenger will go before you; and on day of my visitation, I will visit their sin/misalignment upon them.
YHWH struck the people because they made the calf, which Aaron made.

translation_notes: Golden calf is covenant rupture and misattribution of liberation to a manufactured image.
Aaron's evasive account in v.24 should remain narratively ironic; do not soften his responsibility.
Moses intercession in vv.11-14 and vv.30-32 is central: he argues from YHWH's reputation, promise, and solidarity with the people.
Levite violence in vv.26-29 is severe and ethically dangerous; preserve source wording but flag against modern imitation or sanctification.
Broken tablets symbolize covenant fracture; later renewal must be read through intercession and YHWH's mercy.
safety_note: Do not use Exodus 32 to justify religious violence, purges, or coercive loyalty tests. The restored layer preserves the difficult ancient narrative; ethical application must center intercession, anti-idolatry, covenant rupture, accountability, and YHWH's later mercy proclamation.
